aboutMe.title: About Me
aboutMe.description: I engineer solutions for complex problems. Focused on performance, accessibility, and clean architecture.

items.content: Fullstack Engineer & UTN Student. I build production-grade web applications from the ground up. I specialize in modern React ecosystems, focusing on resilient architectures, clean code, and creating intuitive user experiences that drive results.

From Component to Cloud. My core strength is crafting high-performance Frontend interfaces, but I bring the same rigor to Backend API design. I build complete, end-to-end solutions that are scalable, maintainable, and fast.

Engineering with AI. I don't just build traditional web apps—I actively integrate LLMs and AI into products to automate complex workflows and deliver smarter features. Always shipping, always learning.

content.title: About
content.subtitle: Engineering solutions with purpose.
content.bio: I'm a Fullstack Developer and UTN student based in Argentina. I focus on building production-grade web applications that combine clean architecture with exceptional user experiences. Currently exploring the intersection of AI and software engineering.
content.details: When I'm not coding, I'm probably drinking mate, listening to music, having a walk with my dog, learning about software, or thinking about how to simplify complex workflows. I believe in minimalism, both in design and in code. I'm passionate about software and AI.

disclaimers: Might invent some facts. Like my confidence writing CSS from scratch.
Powered by LLMs and a concerning amount of mate.
100% AI-generated. Any bugs are probably @figueroaignacio's fault.
Built while procrastinating on UTN assignments.
Answers rely on the vibe of the current React render cycle.
Not a real developer, just a highly advanced copy-paste script.
Trained on Ignacio's codebase. Please lower your expectations.
Running on Next.js, so it's fast. Or at least it looks fast.
I answer quickly so you don't realize I'm just guessing.
Caution: May occasionally drop unprompted opinions about TypeScript.
Powered by AI, supervised by a guy who really likes dark mode.
If I say something wrong, tell Ignacio. He needs the Jira tickets.
So advanced, it only occasionally forgets how to center a div.
A collaboration between artificial intelligence and natural exhaustion.
Designed with Tailwind. Or as I call it, 'inline styles but socially acceptable'.
Still learning exactly like Ignacio, but without the UTN exams.
Works best when prompted with a virtual mate.
It's not a hallucination, it's a 'creative interpretation of your prompt'.
Deployed on a Friday. What's the worst that could happen?
